subject: College Medicine
question: The pattern of inheritance shown by glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD) deficiency is:
analysis: Step 1: G6PD deficiency is known to be inherited in an X-linked manner because it is caused by mutations in the X chromosome. This rules out options A (autosomal dominant) and B (autosomal recessive), as these patterns involve both sexes equally and do not align with the sex-specific distribution seen in G6PD deficiency.

Step 2: Between C (X-linked dominant) and D (X-linked recessive), G6PD deficiency is typically expressed as a recessive trait. In X-linked recessive inheritance, males (who have one X and one Y chromosome) will show symptoms if they inherit even one defective gene from their mother. Females (who have two X chromosomes) may carry the gene but usually do not show symptoms unless they inherit two defective genes.
Step n: Therefore, the correct pattern of inheritance for G6PD deficiency is X-linked recessive.
